rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD LEFRAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The attorneys for the petitioner as successor executor request fees for extraordinary services in the total amount of $42,592, in addition to $11,250 previously approved by the Court as partial extraordinary fees payable to the attorneys. The services for which the attorneys are requesting $42,592 on this petition include those related to the decedent's mesothelioma litigation and rendered from 2015 through 2023. The Court's 09/03/2020 order approved payment of $11,250 to the attorneys "as extraordinary fees to which they are entitled through 02/20/2020". According to the attorneys, the Court's 09/03/2020 order fixed fees for their extraordinary services through 02/20/2020, other than those related to the mesothelioma litigation, because the attorneys, in the underlying petition, reserved the right to request additional fees for their litigation-related services rendered before 02/20/2020. The attorneys also point out that the underlying petition did not include their time records in support of the litigation-related services. The Court's 09/03/2020 order has become final and has not been corrected or set aside. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AKIKO SPINDLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/4/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (2) At the end of the third accounting, as shown on the amended Schedule E attached to a "Declaration" filed on 6/7/22, there were 1,507.868 units of Amundi Pioneer Asset Mgmt with a carry value of $48,285.94. This accounting shows 1,292.667 units on hand at the beginning of the accounting with a fiduciary acquisition value of $40,439.71. (3) At the end of the third accounting, there were 2,143.648 units of Fidelity Equity Dividend Income with a CV of $56,258.59. This accounting begins with 2,132.244 units with a fiduciary acquisition value of $55,928.09. (4) At the end of the third accounting, there were 1,000 units of US Treasury Note 2.875% Due 11/30/2025 #4478 - IRA, but this accounting shows 100,000 units with the same carry/fiduciary acquisition value. (5) At the end of the third accounting, there were 700 units of US Treasury Note 2.625% Due 12/31/2025 #4478 - IRA, but this accounting shows 70,000 units of the same with the same carry/fiduciary acquisition value. (6) The market value of the 2,150 units of JP Morgan Chase 4.25% due 10/1/2027 differs from the two market values of the 1,150 units + 1,000 units shown at the beginning of this account. (7) Reinvested shares are usually shown on an informational schedule in a cash accounting, which court-conformed accountings should be. (8) What is meant by "security corrections" shown as receipts? Which securities? (9) There was a disbursement of $413.00 on 9/16/22 and of $288.75 on 3/2/23 for filing fees and two payments of $50 on 10/3/22 and 10/17/22 for "E-Filing Fee", but there was nothing filed around those dates and conservatorship cases are not yet required to e-file. There was also no filing fee of $95 on or around 4/12/23. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/21/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AKIKO SPINDLE 2021 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/4/24. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 3/26/24 shows the incorrect pleading caption. It also does not show whom the attorney represents. (2) A courtesy copy of the third amended accounting petition has not been received by the examiner, so it has not been reviewed fully yet. Furthermore, assets shown in the conservatorship accounting as having been transferred to the trust are not shown in the same order as in the conservatorship accounting or exactly as shown in the conservatorship accounting, so reviewing the accounting is very time-consuming; however, a cursory review online indicated that there might be a few discrepancies between the assets shown in the conservatorship accounting as having been transferred to the trust and the assets shown in the trust accounting as having been received. There are also two transfers in 2023 shown in the conservatorship accounting that occurred after the ending date of the trust accounting, which ended on 6/30/22, despite this being the third amended accounting. Deadline is 5/21/24 for providing the assigned examiner with courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and also, hopefully, a courtesy copy of the third amended petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: A fully amended petition completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends. Please do not recount all the previous filings and continuances.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES LEO NEIRINCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/28/2024. The following procedural issues to be cleared two weeks prior to the date: (1) Need an authority under which the court can grant the statutory compensation based on the value of the property which was not subject to administration. Petition alleges that the funds in IRA account were transferred to the designated beneficiary. If the account had a beneficiary designated, the asset should have not been subject to the administration. (2) Corrected I&A to be filed accordingly. (3) Petition needs to be supplemented to adjust the statutory compensation based on the corrected I&A. The sole heir must be noticed of the info. (4) Appears the attorney requests extraordinary fees for the services she performed in relation to said non-probate assets (IRA). (5) Proposed order to be modified accordingly. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IDA MAE JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Heirship Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/4/24. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The amended petition shows only maternal heirs. What about the paternal heirs? An amended petition completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.3.] (2) The amended petition does not have any documents supporting the claims of heirship. (Exhibit A of the copy provided to the Court is nearly completely illegible. It would be better if the family tree were just done in black and white.) (3) Gregory Payne died on 2/28/21. Who are his successors-in-interest? (4) Children of first cousins are first cousins once removed, not second cousins. (5) The amended petition does not set forth what percentage or fractional share each heir is to receive. (6) The "supplement" filed on 4/8/24, but not e-filed until 4/10/24, AMENDED the [amended] petition to change the percentage interests. The caption of the "supplement" was, therefore, misleading. [CRC 7.3.] (7) Notice is defective if mailed before the e-filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Please provide courtesy copies of a second amended petition that includes ALL heirs and any other supporting papers, such as proofs of service (and a revised proposed order, if applicable) to the assigned examiner by 5/21/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])
